name: Litoraleño (Guaranític) Spanish

ancestors.notes: General Latin American Spanish (koine) — seseo/yeismo baseline shared by all LatAm varieties (Lipski 1994).
Guaraní indigenous adstrate (regional contact influence; see stub gn.json).

notes: STRUCTURAL STUB. Litoraleño / Guaranític Spanish of north-eastern Argentina (Corrientes, Misiones) — Lipski's (1994) 'Paraguay, north-eastern Argentina, eastern Bolivia' zone; strong Guaraní adstrate. Only the pan-Latin-American baseline (seseo: c/z->/s/, yeismo: ll->/ʝ/; Lipski 1994) is modelled here; the dialect-specific phonology is deliberately not yet described (stub tier). Ancestry: es-ES-x-medieval -> es-ES -> es-419 -> es-AR -> es-AR-x-litoral, with the indigenous adstrate(s) Guaraní.

sources.author: Lipski, John M.
sources.title: Geographical and Social Varieties of Spanish: An Overview
sources.publisher: In J.I. Hualde, A. Olarrea & E. O'Rourke (eds.), The Handbook of Hispanic Linguistics, Wiley-Blackwell
sources.notes: READ (geo.pdf). Reproduces the Lipski (1994) geographical dialect-zone classification of Latin American Spanish (list (2)): Mexican/SW-US; Caribbean; Guatemala-Yucatan-Costa Rica; El Salvador-Honduras-Nicaragua; interior Colombia + highland Venezuela; Pacific coast Colombia/Ecuador/Peru; Andean (Ecuador/Peru/Bolivia/NW-Argentina/NE-Chile); Chile; Paraguay/NE-Argentina/E-Bolivia; Argentina/Uruguay. Used here to enumerate the regional zones; no phonology quoted.

sources.author: Lipski, John M.
sources.title: Latin American Spanish
sources.notes: The comprehensive dialect survey underlying the zone classification (cited via the author's own overview, geo.pdf, which was read).

location.notes: Corrientes — a representative city of the Guaraní-contact Litoral (Corrientes and Misiones). [Wikidata Q44211 P625]
